fields.description:
## Description

**What**: Rewrite merge-to-main.sh (or replacement) as a PR-based merge primitive: create PR, poll CI, merge via GitHub API, update local worktree. Detect and error on the old direct-push path. The primitive is invoked per-batch by sprint (87ab-a487) and once at session-close by end-session.
**Why**: The GitHub Ruleset blocks direct pushes to main. The merge primitive must create PRs and wait for CI enforcement before merging.
**Scope**:
- IN: PR creation (gh pr create), CI polling (gh pr checks / gh run watch), merge (gh pr merge with auto flag), local worktree update to origin/main, old-path detection with clear error, edge case handling (422 stale HEAD, cli/cli 8206/8514), idempotent re-entry so sprint --resume can re-attach to an in-flight PR rather than creating a duplicate
- OUT: Sprint's per-batch loop logic (87ab-a487), hook removal (5428-bebd), documentation updates (ed9c-a277), CI workflow creation (3aaa-0238)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, running the merge primitive creates a PR, waits for CI to pass, merges via the GitHub API, and updates the local worktree to origin/main
  ← Satisfies SC4: "revised merge-to-main.sh implements PR workflow"
- When this story is complete, existing worktree sessions attempting the old direct-push path receive a non-zero exit with actionable guidance directing them to the PR workflow
  ← Satisfies SC6: "existing worktree sessions receive clear error message"
- When this story is complete, the merge primitive handles gh pr merge auto edge cases (stale HEAD 422, failed check errors) with explicit error detection and retry logic
  ← Satisfies SC4
- When this story is complete, the primitive is idempotent on re-entry — invoking it for a branch that already has an open PR re-attaches and resumes wait-on-CI rather than creating a duplicate PR
  ← Supports SC9 (sprint --resume per-batch correctness, owned by 87ab-a487)
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Conflicting Assumptions] Current merge-to-main.sh phases include version_bump which creates a local commit on main. PR-based flow cannot create local commits on main — the merge happens remotely. Decide: pre-merge version bump on branch? post-merge CI Action? Skip version bump in PR flow?
- [Consumer Impact] end-session SKILL.md calls merge-to-main.sh with --bump flag. Sprint per-batch caller (87ab-a487) calls it without --bump. Verify the new interface supports both call sites, especially --bump, --resume, and the is_merged check in worktree cleanup.
- [Reliability] gh pr merge auto has documented failure modes (cli/cli 8206, 8514). Must implement explicit error detection and retry.
- [Scope Overlap] Both this story and Story 5428-bebd modify COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md. Coordinate changes to prevent merge conflicts — 5428-bebd removes gate steps, this story updates the "After Commit: Merging to Main" section.
- [Interface contract] The primitive's call shape is consumed by 87ab-a487 (per-batch) and end-session (epic-close). Define and document the contract before 87ab-a487 implementation begins.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
